article.title: July 5th, 2028: permanent underclass
article.preview_text:
July 5th 2028 POV:
it is monday morning. your personal agent wakes you up using your Alexa-like smart device built by a 19 years old dropout and shows you your stats Whoop AI. you check your sleep

blocks.text: July 5th 2028 POV:

it is monday morning. your personal agent wakes you up using your Alexa-like smart device built by a 19 years old dropout and shows you your stats Whoop AI. you check your sleep score, it’s 95%, but you still cannot get out of your bed. you have been able to sleep very well, but your energy feels down. you feel massive mind fog, a bit of anxiety. it’s been 30mins, but you are still in your bed thinking about the recent @Citrini7 article you just read yesterday night.

you don't have a job. you have been looking for one since you got laid off in 2027 crash. your job is automated by ai agents now, but you have some savings to live off along with some stimulus check you received from President Trump ($5000 stimulus check for people who lost their jobs in 2027).
and then you question yourself, why didn't you make it, and how did the world end up like that?

well, you see, it all started when you got excited about ai agents back in january 2026. you were claude-maxxing, then shifted to gpt. you were coding like you are Elliot from Mr. Robot or Gilfoyle from Silicon Valley. you could build anything. you worked on your unfinished projects, built multiple Claude skills, built softwares that got insane twitter engagement. hell, you even got a reply from Pete, founder of OpenClaw, and Elon Musk on your X tweets.
but that's all you could do.

you thought you are better than your peers because you could ship code a bit faster than them and had over 10k commits on github in 2026, thanks to $200 plan of Claude and OpenAI. but none of it mattered because your company had mass layoffs. you looked around and every company was in the similar spot.

By the end of 2026, a lot of your friends got laid off. these folks were at Google, Oracle, Amazon, Microsoft, Netflix and every other big company you could think of. You were doing great at DoorDash as well. you got promoted earlier that year, and you thought you are on the path to be a staff engineer working on some bigger projects. But by the end of 2026, DoorDash revenue dropped significantly.

It wasn't your mistake. The Habitual app loyalty just wasn't built for machines and ai agents that people were running on their local machines. the agents constantly looked for cheaper options for their "humans," and hence, DoorDash lost its edge to newly vibecoded delivery apps built by 17-year-old devs.

if you looked around, almost everything and every company was using ai agents and LLMs by the end of 2026. these agents coded better than you. they did better bug squash than you and didn't need family time like you did. the design spec that took you a week was done in 1 hour now by design-architect-agent. at first, it was an advantage to you, an upper hand, over your peers. But then your peers started using it, and everyone else did too.

There was no objective performance left on how great of an engineer you were. your ai agent did 200 commits in a week alone for your company. And so, eventually, you lost your job, along with your team. only the higher ups of DoorDash survived because they were the ones instructing these ai agents after all.

your wife is a NURSE in a nearby hospital. She was supposed to go to med school in 2026, but then she needed to work a few more weeks while you find a job. Naturally, her job became ever more important. So much important that you guys don't get enough time to see each other, but that is fine because you have your OpenClaw to talk to you every minute.
you think about the feedback loop for AI and Citrni's article again. it turns out that everything written in the Citrini's article is true.

it's already 11am, and you muster some energy to get out of the bed. your wife is already at work. you decide to read a book while you pour yourself a coffee. you get a text from your ai bot on iMessage. it tells you that there are some blue collar jobs paying close to 90k a year.

you browse through the message that is perfectly crafted by your bot. it has jobs, in order: constructor manger, city development manger, solutions architect, lumberjack, and recreation center manager. you ask it to apply to all of them using your updated resume and contact info.

4 hours later, you get a notification, read by your agent: “Our job agent has shortlisted your resume, and we’d like to interview you for city development manger. the salary is $80k a year with 15 hours/day work shift.”

"it is not much, but it's honest work," you think to yourself.

so you schedule the call for the next day and began prepping. meanwhile, you tell your agent to build you a handbook for job interview questions and apply to more jobs, while you finish your lunch and come back from walking your dog.

you get done with house chores, dog walk and get done with a pickleball game with your friends. they have similar jobs and they tell you that it's cool that you have an interview the next day. you are optimistic about it.
next day, you are ready to be interviewed for the first round, and you know who is going to interview you. it's an ai agent.
It asks basic questions:
“What are your salary expectations?”
“Tell me about your previous work.”
“Why did you apply for this job?”
you are hesitant to say that you couldn’t find a job for a while, but you are honest so you tell your reasons either way.

you think you nailed the interview, and then you go do more chores around the house, while your trading bot prints $100 in pnl that day. the sad thing about a trading bot is that everyone has a similar bot, and you don’t have an edge to trade. but, oh well, it’s still some money that helps in paying bills.
by the end of next day, you receive a rejection email from the job. it is an automated email by the same ai agent and it read: “the agent matched a better person for the job.”

you are in despair, but somewhere in your heart you expected this scenario. you cook dinner so that your wife and you can, at least, eat together in a long time. she is coming home early that evening after hearing about your rejection.

you got the recipe from your agent, and you start cooking the dinner. you look around you. everything is rented or on a lease: your apartment, your car, your furniture, your land. there is nothing that YOU OWN, except for some physical gold, an old rolex watch, some stocks, and some crypto coins along a few more belongings. you don’t have much to your name, but you are a bit content, a bit grateful for a loving wife, a dog, and maybe a kid soon.

and then you remember about the conspiracy theory and this phrase by WEF and global elites: “you’ll own nothing, and you’ll be happy.” the theory first appeared in 2016 when global elites started talking about the “Rentier” Economy, where you don’t buy anything, but rather lease everything. only the global elites truly “own” things, and they can take it away from you any day they wish.

Everything belongs to BlackRock and all similar firms. you sold last piece of land that your granddad left you when you needed money. but you can’t do much about it, right? you needed the money that time. you still need to find a job because you just got rejected by one today, and you have bills to pay. so what’s the point of thinking all of it. you are happy where you are, and that’s the main purpose of life as suggested by many self-help gurus like Garyvee and Jay Shetty. they said you have a whole life to figure out things and who cares what others say or think. so you dont care either.

But something deep in your heart speaks up while you wait for the food to cook. Your heart sinks. It misses a beat. And then you realize that after three years, you still couldn’t make it out. And sadly, achingly, you will never escape the permanent underclass.
